blocks:
Ayahs

وَٱلْفَجْرِ (۱)
By the Dawn, (1)
وَلَيَالٍ عَشْرٍ (۲)
by the ten nights, (2)
وَٱلشَّفْعِ وَٱلْوَتْرِ (۳)
by the Even and the Odd, (3)
وَٱلَّيْلِ إِذَا يَسْرِ (٤)
by the night when it departs! (4)
هَلْ فِى ذَٰلِكَ قَسَمٌ لِّذِى حِجْرٍ (٥)
Is there an oath in that for one possessing intellect? (5)

Key Points

"Fajr" (الفجر) means splitting, and what is meant by it is the splitting of darkness by the dawn, which is a sacred and valuable time. It is the time when creatures become active and begin a new morning and day. In a hadith, the meaning of Fajr is considered to be Imam Mahdi عليه السلام: «الفجر هو القائم علیه السلام» (Fajr is the Qā’im عليه السلام), by whose rising the dark night of oppression and corruption will be swept away. (34)

"Hijr" (حِجر) means prevention, and what is meant by it is the intellect (Aql) which prevents man from wrongdoing. Just as "Mahjūr" (محجور) means legally restricted (forbidden from disposing of property).

In narrations, many instances have been mentioned for "Shaf’" (شَفع), meaning even (pair), and "Witr" (وَتر), meaning odd (single). Among these are that Witr refers to the ninth day of Dhul-Hijjah (the Day of Arafah), and Shaf’ refers to the tenth day of Dhul-Hijjah (Eid al-Adha). Or that Shaf’ refers to the two mountains of Safa and Marwah, and Witr refers to the Ka’bah, which is one. Or that Witr refers to the One God (Allah), and Shaf’ refers to the creatures, all of whom are pairs (couples). Or it refers to the recommended prayers of Shaf’ and Witr which are recited at dawn (Sahar). Or that Shaf’ refers to the ninth and tenth days of Dhul-Hijjah, and Witr refers to the night of Mash’ar. (35)

Time is very valuable, indeed sacred, and we should not waste it. Allah has sworn by all parts of time: «والصبح» (36) (Swearing by the morning), «والضُحى» (37) (Swearing by the forenoon), «والنهار» (38) (Swearing by the day), «والعصر» (39) (Swearing by the afternoon/age).

Interestingly, He has sworn by the dawn (Sahar) three times: «والّیل اذا یَسر» (40), «والّیل اذا عَسعس» (41), «والّیل اذ أدبر» (42) (Swearing by the night when it is drawing to a close, meaning swearing by the time of dawn/Sahar.)

What is meant by «لیال عَشر» (ten nights) is either the last ten nights of the blessed month of Ramadan, which include the Nights of Qadr, or the first ten nights of the month of Dhul-Hijjah, which encompass the Day of Arafah and Eid al-Adha.

34) Tafsir Burhan. 35) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 36) Mudaththir, 34. 37) Duha, 1. 38) Shams, 3. 39) Asr, 1. 40) Fajr, 4. 41) Takwir, 17. 42) Mudaththir, 33.

Messages

1- Time is sacred and worthy of oath. «والفجر»

2- Certain days possess special sanctity. «ولیال عشر»

3- The final hours of the night, during which spiritual wayfarers undertake their spiritual journey, possess a special sanctity. «و الّیل اذا یسر»

4- The addressees of the Quran are people of intellect. «لذى حجر»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Fajr

This Surah has thirty verses and was revealed in Mecca. Its name is derived from the first verse and means "The Dawn." Like other Meccan Surahs, its verses are short, forceful, and warning.

This Surah has several sections, beginning with oaths by times such as the night, the dawn (Sahar), and the daybreak (Fajr), and continues with a reference to the history of rebellious and tyrannical nations.

The tradition of trial and testing (Ibtilā) by Allah and the varying reactions of man towards it, as well as man's presence in the court of the Day of Judgment, the expression of regret and remorse by the impure, and the manifestation of joy and satisfaction by the pure, constitute the remainder of the verses of this Surah.

Ayahs

أَلَمْ تَرَ كَيْفَ فَعَلَ رَبُّكَ بِعَادٍ (٦)
Have you not regarded how your Lord dealt with [the people of] ‘Ad, (6)
إِرَمَ ذَاتِ ٱلْعِمَادِ (۷)
[and] Iram, [the city] of the pillars, (7)
ٱلَّتِى لَمْ يُخْلَقْ مِثْلُهَا فِى ٱلْبِلَـٰدِ (۸)
the like of which was not created among cities, (8)
وَثَمُودَ ٱلَّذِينَ جَابُوا۟ ٱلصَّخْرَ بِٱلْوَادِ (۹)
and [the people of] Thamud, who hollowed out the rocks in the valley, (9)
وَفِرْعَوْنَ ذِى ٱلْأَوْتَادِ (۱٠)
and Pharaoh, the impaler (10)
ٱلَّذِينَ طَغَوْا۟ فِى ٱلْبِلَـٰدِ (۱۱)
—those who rebelled [against Allah] in their cities (11)
فَأَكْثَرُوا۟ فِيهَا ٱلْفَسَادَ (۱۲)
and caused much corruption in them, (12)
فَصَبَّ عَلَيْهِمْ رَبُّكَ سَوْطَ عَذَابٍ (۱۳)
so your Lord poured on them lashes of punishment. (13)
إِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَبِٱلْمِرْصَادِ (۱٤)
Indeed your Lord is in ambush. (14)

Key Points

The people of 'Ad were two groups: the ancient 'Ad (Ad al-Ula) and the later 'Ad, based on the verse "عادٍ الاولى" (Ad al-Ula), who lived in the Al-Ahqaf region after Prophet Nuh (Noah). They possessed large physiques, immense power, and prosperous cities. "اِرَم" (Iram) is either another name for the tribe of 'Ad, the name of a branch of the people of 'Ad, or the name of the region where the people of 'Ad lived, whose buildings had large and tall pillars. Some consider Iram to be the structure built by Shaddad, and others believe it was built by Alexander in Alexandria. Sakhra (صَخر) is the plural of sakhrah, meaning large, hard stones. Wad (واد) refers to the distance between a mountain and a hill. Imad (عماد) means a pillar or a tall building. Thamud (ثمود) refers to the pre-historic Arabs who resided in northern Arabia, a people whose Prophet was Salih (عليه السلام). Jaboo (جابوا) means to cut stone. The word jawab (جواب), which derives from this root, means to cut off a question with a correct answer. Apparently, the people of Thamud came after the people of 'Ad, which is why their name is mentioned after them. The wrath upon 'Ad and Thamud is an example of Allah being in ambush (lying in wait) for the oppressors. Allah is in ambush, meaning wrongdoers have no escape route and will be suddenly seized. The solidity of buildings does not prevent divine eradication. One should not feel secure from Allah's wrath merely because of the strength of their structures. The intent of Dhul-Awtad (ذو الاوتاد) is either that the foundations of Pharaoh's government were firm, or that he would nail his opponents (crucify them), or that he possessed vast armies. The word sabba (صَبَّ) means to pour. The expression of the pouring down of punishment is perhaps a metaphor for continuous and successive torment. (43) How could Allah not be in ambush when He is closer to us than the jugular vein? "و نحن اقرب الیه من حبل الورید" (44)

43) Tafsir Rahnama. 44) Qaf, 16.

Messages

1- Do not forget the history of the eradication of transgressors. "اَلم تَر"

2- Destroying the rebellious is a manifestation of Allah's Lordship. "فعل ربّك بعاد" As Sa'di said: Showing mercy to the sharp-toothed leopard is oppression against the sheep.

3- The sign of wisdom and intellect is taking lessons from history. "لذى حجر الم تَر كیف فعل ربّك"

4- The Noble Prophet (صلى الله علیه وآله) was informed of the history of the people of 'Ad, Thamud, and Pharaoh through revelation. "الم تَر"

5- Architectural innovation using stone existed among the people of 'Ad. "لم یخلق مثلها فى البلاد"

6- Intellectual disagreement and conflict should not prevent the acknowledgment of the art and innovations of others. "لم یخلق مثلها فى البلاد"

7- Power without spirituality is a factor that disrupts security. "جابوا الصخر...ذى الاوتاد....طغوا فى البلاد"

8- The transgressor and transgression recognize no boundaries. "طغوا فى البلاد" (The intent of al-bilad is all regions they had access to.)

9- The source of ruin and corruption are the tyrants (taghuts). "طغوا فى البلاد فاكثروا فیها الفساد"

10- Civilization and progress, if not accompanied by piety and righteousness, are factors leading to downfall and destruction. "فصب علیهم ربّك سوط عذاب"

11- A trainer (or Lord) sometimes needs a whip for discipline. "ربُّك سوط"

12- Transgression and corruption are not removed except through wrath. "طغوا... سوط عذاب"

13- Do not let respites make you arrogant, for God is in ambush. "ان ربّك لبالمرصاد"

14- Worldly punishment, compared to the torment of the Hereafter, is like a whip whose pain is temporary and whose effects are superficial, remaining only on the skin. "سوط عذاب"

Ayahs

فَأَمَّا ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ إِذَا مَا ٱبْتَلَىٰهُ رَبُّهُۥ فَأَكْرَمَهُۥ وَنَعَّمَهُۥ فَيَقُولُ رَبِّىٓ أَكْرَمَنِ (۱٥)
As for man, whenever his Lord tests him, and grants him honour, and blesses him, he says, ‘My Lord has honoured me.’ (15)
وَأَمَّآ إِذَا مَا ٱبْتَلَىٰهُ فَقَدَرَ عَلَيْهِ رِزْقَهُۥ فَيَقُولُ رَبِّىٓ أَهَـٰنَنِ (۱٦)
But when He tests him and tightens for him his provision, he says, ‘My Lord has humiliated me.’ (16)
كَلَّا ۖ بَل لَّا تُكْرِمُونَ ٱلْيَتِيمَ (۱۷)
No indeed! No, you do not honour the orphan, (17)
وَلَا تَحَـٰٓضُّونَ عَلَىٰ طَعَامِ ٱلْمِسْكِينِ (۱۸)
and do not urge the feeding of the needy. (18)
وَتَأْكُلُونَ ٱلتُّرَاثَ أَكْلًا لَّمًّا (۱۹)
You eat the inheritance rapaciously, (19)
وَتُحِبُّونَ ٱلْمَالَ حُبًّا جَمًّا (۲٠)
and love wealth with much fondness. (20)

Key Points

"أكلاً لمّا" means eating everything at once, without paying attention to whether it is lawful (halal) or unlawful (haram), or whether the property of others has been added to it or not. "Lamman" (لمّا) is the verbal noun (masdar) of "lamamtu" (لَمَمت), meaning to gather and annex. "Jamman" (جَمّا) means abundant and heaped up. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: I and anyone who takes an orphan under his guardianship will be side by side in Paradise. (45) One of the definite traditions and programs of Allah is the testing of people; not, of course, for the purpose of knowing them, but for the manifestation of their characteristics and the emergence of their efforts, so that He may grant reward based on those actions. Divine tests are varied; sometimes He tests man with prosperity and wealth, and sometimes with poverty and difficulties. But the hasty human being, who is heedless of the Divine test and its objectives, resorts to wrong judgments, analyses, and interpretations, and complains against Allah.

45) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

  1. Divine tests are a manifestation of the Lord being in wait for man. «انّ ربّك لبالمرصاد فامّا الانسان اذا ما ابتلاه...»
  2. Testing is a means of nurturing and growth for man. «الانسان ابتلاه ربّه»
  3. Man's blessings are based on His grace, not on our merit. Therefore, do not fall into arrogance and conceit, and do not be heedless of the test. «اكرمه و نعّمه»
  4. Neither poverty is a sign of humiliation nor wealth a sign of honor; rather, both are means of testing. «ابتلاه... فاكرمه، ابتلاه فقدر علیه رزقه»
  5. More important than the orphan's body is his soul, which must be honored. «تكرمون»
  6. Compensate for spiritual deficiencies with respect and affection. (The first expectation of orphans is respect for their personality.) «تكرمون الیتیم»
  7. Do not make inappropriate judgments regarding the actions of Allah. Do not assume the reason for the scarcity of provision is Allah's indifference, but rather know it is the result of your own performance. «كلاّ بل لاتكرمون الیتیم»
  8. Benevolence towards others plays an important role in man's provision and sustenance. «لاتكرمون - فقدر علیه رزقه»
  9. The destitute should not remain hungry. They must be cared for through one's own spending (infaq) and by encouraging others. «تحاضّون على طعام المسكین»
  10. Be careful about your earnings; not every morsel is permissible to consume. «اكلاً لّما»
  11. The desire for wealth is innate (fitrah), but what is blameworthy is intense desire. «حُبّاً جمّاً»
  12. Allah's honoring of you should be the cause of your honoring of orphans. «اكرمه... لا تكرمون»

Ayahs

كَلَّآ إِذَا دُكَّتِ ٱلْأَرْضُ دَكًّا دَكًّا (۲۱)
No indeed! When the earth is levelled to a plain, (21)
وَجَآءَ رَبُّكَ وَٱلْمَلَكُ صَفًّا صَفًّا (۲۲)
and your Lord [’s edict] arrives with the angels in ranks, (22)
وَجِا۟ىٓءَ يَوْمَئِذٍۭ بِجَهَنَّمَ ۚ يَوْمَئِذٍ يَتَذَكَّرُ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ وَأَنَّىٰ لَهُ ٱلذِّكْرَىٰ (۲۳)
the day when hell is brought [near], man will take admonition on that day, but what will that admonition avail him? (23)
يَقُولُ يَـٰلَيْتَنِى قَدَّمْتُ لِحَيَاتِى (۲٤)
He will say, ‘Alas, had I sent ahead for my life [in the Hereafter]!’ (24)
فَيَوْمَئِذٍ لَّا يُعَذِّبُ عَذَابَهُۥٓ أَحَدٌ (۲٥)
On that day none shall punish as He punishes, (25)
وَلَا يُوثِقُ وَثَاقَهُۥٓ أَحَدٌ (۲٦)
and none shall bind as He binds. (26)

Key Points

"دكّ" (Dakk) means crushing and leveling. Dakkah (platform) and Dukkan (shop) are places where the ground is leveled and prepared for displaying goods. Imam Riḍā عليه السلام said: The meaning of "جاء ربُّك" (Your Lord comes) is the coming of the command of the Lord (not the coming of the Lord Himself).(46) Just as He says in Ayah 33 of Sūrat An-Naḥl: "یأتى امر ربّك" (The command of your Lord comes). The meaning of "و جیى ء یومئذ بجهنّم" (and Hell is brought forth that Day) is the lifting of the veil from it.(47) Just as He says elsewhere: "برّزت الجحیم لمن یرى" (The Blazing Fire is made manifest for whoever sees).(48) When the Ayah "و جیى ء یومئذ بجهنّم" was revealed, the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was so overwhelmed that no one dared to speak to him.(49)

(46) Tafsīr Nūr ath-Thaqalayn. (47) Tafsīr Al-Mīzān. (48) An-Nāzi‘āt, 36. (49) Biḥār, Vol. 105, p. 103.

Messages

1- Allah restrains man from intense love of wealth and usurping the rights of others. "تحبّون المال - كلاّ..."

2- The cure for greed and love of wealth is the remembrance of the Day of Judgment. "تأكلون التراث... تحبّون المال... كلاّ اذا دكّت الارض"

3- The Day of Judgment is the day of the absolute sovereignty of the Divine command. "جاء ربّك"

4- The display of the angels' power on the Day of Judgment is evident through their adherence to order and hierarchy. "الملك صفّا صفّا"

5- Remembrance and regret on the Day of Judgment are useless. "و انّى له الذّكرى"

6- The Day of Judgment is the day of awareness and remembrance. "یومئذ یتذكّر الانسان"

7- Hell will appear on the Day of Judgment. "جیى ء... بجهنّم"

8- The Day of Judgment is the day of regret. "یا لیتنى"

9- Wishing is not effective; effort is necessary. "یا لیتنى قدّمت"

10- The real and eternal life is the life of the Hereafter. "لحیاتى"

11- The Merciful God is severe when appropriate. "لایعذّب عذابه احد"

12- While there might be similarities to Allah's punishments in this world, the Divine punishment in the Hereafter has no parallel. "فیومئذ لا یعذّب عذابه احد"

13- On the Day of Judgment, there is no possibility of escaping the punishment. "لا یعذّب... لا یوثق"

Ayahs

يَـٰٓأَيَّتُهَا ٱلنَّفْسُ ٱلْمُطْمَئِنَّةُ (۲۷)
‘O soul at peace! (27)
ٱرْجِعِىٓ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكِ رَاضِيَةً مَّرْضِيَّةً (۲۸)
Return to your Lord, pleased and pleasing! (28)
فَٱدْخُلِى فِى عِبَـٰدِى (۲۹)
Then enter among My servants, (29)
وَٱدْخُلِى جَنَّتِى (۳٠)
and enter My paradise!’ (30)

Key Points

A tranquil heart and the contented soul (Nafs al-Mutma'innah) are attained through the remembrance (Dhikr) of Allah. «الا بذكراللّه تطمئنّ القلوب» (50) And the best remembrance of Allah is prayer (Salat). «اقم الصّلاة لذكرى» (51) Being pleased (radi) and being pleasing (mardhi) are signs of the contented soul, meaning that it is pleased with Allah, and Allah is also pleased with it. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was asked whether the believer is pleased with giving up his soul (dying). He replied: In the first moment, he is not pleased, but the Angel of Death (Qabid ar-Ruh) comforts him, saying, "I am kinder to you than a father; open your eyes." He opens his eyes and sees the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, and Ali, and Fatimah, and Hasan, and Husayn, and the rest of the Imams علیهم السلام. The angel says: "These are your friends." The believer becomes pleased upon seeing this scene, and at this moment, he hears the address, «یا ایّتها النفس المطمئنّة». (52)

51) Taha, 14. 52) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- The essence of man lies with the soul (Ruh), and the body is its vehicle. «یا ایّتها النفس» (This is Allah's address to the soul.)

2- Paradise without companions and friends is not Paradise. «اُدخلى فى عبادى»

3- Paradise has types and degrees. «جنّتى» Just as the Quran has stated in multiple instances: «جنّات عدن»
